channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: MAHA Is Still Creepily Obsessed With Your Fertility
item.description: RFK Jr. and Dr. Mehmet Oz’s comments about teen sperm count and “underbabied” Americans at a recent women’s health event underscore the White House’s pronatalist agenda.

item.title: Meta won’t let you block its AI account on Threads
item.description: Meta announced on Tuesday that it's testing a Threads feature that lets users tag a Meta AI account to get answers to questions or context about a conversation on the platform. If you've spent any time looking at replies on X as of late, this new feature sounds a lot like Meta's take on people […]

item.title: NBA The Run hits the streets on June 9
item.description: The game is sort of a spiritual sequel to NBA Street and NBA Jam.

item.title: Samsung's One UI 9 beta will roll out for Galaxy S26 owners this week
item.description: However, its marquee features won't arrive until the final release.

item.title: The $5.5 trillion talent crisis starts in kindergarten
item.description: Here’s why the talent pipeline is failing long before job training even starts. A few years ago, I sat across from the CEO of a Fortune 500 company who told me, “We can’t find people who can solve problems.” When I asked him where he thought the issue began, he answered, “Somewhere in college, I guess.”

item.title: The Audemars Piguet x Swatch Collaboration Is Here for Real
item.description: With Instagram awash with inaccurate AI-generated concepts for its Royal Pop collab, Swatch was forced to reveal the genuine article.

item.title: Metroid Dread developer MercurySteam announces layoffs
item.description: The Spanish studio's newest title is due to be released on Steam later this week.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang to new grads: ‘Run, don’t walk’ toward AI
item.description: During a commencement speech at Carnegie Mellon University, Huang said that now is the best time to start a career. Jensen Huang left Carnegie Mellon University’s class of 2026 with a message that pushed back against graduation-season anxiety: there’s no better time than now to be starting a career.

item.title: How to watch Lazio vs. Inter in the Coppa Italia final online for free
item.description: How to watch Coppa Italia final for free. Live stream Lazio vs. Inter in the 2026 Coppa Italia final for free.

item.title: Meta will tell parents when their teens add new interests to their Instagram algorithm
item.description: Starting Tuesday, parents can get more details about what their teens are up to on Instagram. Meta is adding a feature to its Teen Accounts that shows parents the "general topics their teens engage with," like "basketball" or "fashion." Meta also says it will soon start notifying parents when their teen adds a new interest […]

item.title: Western Digital says it can increase your HDD capacity and cut power usage thanks to a 'clever way' of spinning down hard drives that doesn't impact performance too much
item.description: Western Digital develops hard drives which spin down for lower power consumption while maintaining performance acceptable for hyperscale production workloads.

item.title: Homebuilders in Sun Belt housing markets are working through a ‘spec overhang’
item.description: Homebuilders spent much of the past year cutting prices, upping incentives, cutting specs, and slowing starts in the softest Sun Belt markets. Now, unsold completed new-build inventory has stopped surging. Want more housing market stories from Lance Lambert’s ResiClub in your inbox? Subscribe to the ResiClub newsletter.

item.title: Japan’s biggest snack maker is changing its iconic chip bags because of a growing global crisis
item.description: Calbee says shortages tied to the Strait of Hormuz blockade are forcing some of its best-known products into monochrome packaging. Known for their unique flavors and vibrant designs, Japanese snacks are coveted around the world. But now, thanks to geopolitical tensions, one of Japan’s biggest snack makers is deciding to dial back its vibrant packaging, at least temporarily....

item.title: Amazon launches 30-minute delivery in dozens of cities
item.description: Amazon is rolling out Amazon Now across the U.S., giving users the ability to get what they need in half an hour.

item.title: Meta made $14 million just off of these scam ads
item.description: A new report shows Meta failed to curb scam ads targeting seniors and Medicare recipients, instead bringing in millions of advertising revenue from their sale.

item.title: 9 000 amas stellaires observés : les télescopes James-Webb et Hubble révèlent comment les étoiles s’en libèrent
item.description: En observant des milliers de jeunes amas stellaires grâce aux télescopes James-Webb et Hubble, des astronomes ont découvert que les amas les plus massifs dissipent leur nuage de gaz (nébuleuse) beaucoup plus rapidement que prévu. Une avancée qui éclaire les premières étapes de la naissance des...

item.title: Iran Is Using Tiny ‘Mosquito’ Boats to Shut Down the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran’s traditional naval fleet has been almost completely destroyed by US-Israeli raids. But Iran’s military has put a fleet of small vessels on the water that is crippling every passageway.

item.title: Tekken director Katsuhiro Harada is back with his own studio under SNK
item.description: Six months after leaving Bandai Namco, Harada is in charge of his own team.

item.title: Google’s new Create My Widget tool is ‘the first step in generative UI’ — and as an iPhone user, I’m not sure how iOS 27 can compete with Android 17
item.description: Google has announced a swath of new Android 17 features, one of which is an AI-powered widget builder.

item.title: Google is reportedly considering working with SpaceX on orbital data centers
item.description: The company has been working on the idea since the end of 2025.
